disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High-impact blunt trauma to the anterior right thigh from striking a solid table edge approximately 3 hours ago.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I walked really hard into the corner of my heavy oak table about three hours ago and caught my thigh right on the edge.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid focal swelling and a firm localised lump directly over the impact site on the anterior thigh.
trigger_question_examples: What does the injured area look and feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's a hard, swollen lump right where I hit it — it came up really fast, within a few minutes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Early red-purple ecchymosis developing over the impact site.
trigger_question_examples: Has the skin changed colour over the area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, a red-purple bruise is starting to come up over the sore lump.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient retains full weight-bearing capacity and can walk, albeit with an antalgic limp.
trigger_question_examples: Can you stand and put weight on that leg?
Are you able to walk on it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I can still stand and walk on it — I've just got a bit of a limp because it's sore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No distal numbness, tingling or paraesthesia in the leg or foot (neurovascular screen currently negative).
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness, tingling or pins and needles in your leg or foot?
Does your foot feel normal — any loss of sensation?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no pins and needles or numbness — my foot feels normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The whole thigh/leg is not uniformly tight, tense or rock-hard — swelling is localised to the impact site, and pain is not out of proportion to the injury.
trigger_question_examples: Is your whole leg becoming tight, hard or swollen, or is it just the one spot?
Is the pain far worse than you'd expect for a knock — a deep, relentless, spreading pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's really just the one spot that's swollen and hard — the rest of my leg feels normal and the pain is sore but not unbearable.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The overlying skin is intact with no laceration or open wound.
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin broken or cut anywhere over the area?
Is there any open wound or bleeding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the skin's all intact — no cut or graze, just the bruise coming up underneath.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Self-care already taken (20 minutes of ice); no regular medications and no known allergies.
trigger_question_examples: Have you done anything for it so far?
Are you on any regular medications or have any allergies?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I put an ice pack on for about 20 minutes. I don't take any regular medicines and I've got no allergies.
